text.title: web-scraping-agentic-workflow Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for web-scraping-agentic-workflow. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: A scrape agent MUST execute in this order: (1) curl -I to confirm SSR vs JS-render; (2) choose httpx+selectolax for SSR, Playwright for JS; (3) extract with role/text locators; (4) validate every row with Pydantic/Zod and log invalid count; (5) write to raw/YYYY-MM-DD/source.jsonl; (6) on schema drift (>5% field disappearance), stop and raise a human review issue.
rule.rationale: Each step is a quality gate. Skipping the SSR probe wastes browser resources. Skipping validation lets corrupt rows contaminate the dataset silently. Skipping drift detection makes pipeline failures invisible for days.

rule.statement: Always check robots.txt before scraping and cache the result for the run. Identify your bot with a descriptive User-Agent including a contact email. Snapshot raw HTML alongside parsed JSON. Validate rows with Pydantic/Zod and log validation errors to a separate stream. For LLM consumption, prefer Firecrawl or r.jina.ai markdown output — it saves 70% or more tokens versus raw HTML.
rule.rationale: robots.txt compliance reduces ban risk and is a legal shield in many jurisdictions. Raw HTML snapshots let you re-parse without re-scraping when the parser changes. Pydantic validation surfaces schema drift at ingestion rather than at query time.

rule.statement: In agent prompts, robots.txt check MUST be the first step — agents skip it unless explicitly instructed. Provide the actual DOM selector from inspection, not a guessed class name. Specify pagination layout explicitly (next-button or infinite-scroll) since agents default to the wrong one.
rule.rationale: LLMs hallucinate selectors that "feel right" (.product-card) when the actual class is .css-1abc23. "Just retry on failure" loops without backoff get IPs banned in minutes. Login flows produce cookies/tokens that agents commit unless explicitly gitignored.
